workouts.name: Barbell Bench Press
workouts.description: The king of chest exercises. Builds mass and strength across the entire chest.
workouts.form: Lie flat on bench. Grip bar slightly wider than shoulder-width. Lower to mid-chest. Press explosively upward. Keep feet flat, back slightly arched.
workouts.mistakes: Flaring elbows too wide, bouncing bar off chest, lifting hips off bench, uneven grip.

workouts.name: Pull-Up
workouts.description: The ultimate bodyweight back builder. Develops width and thickness simultaneously.
workouts.form: Hang with arms fully extended. Pull elbows down and back. Chin clears bar. Lower slowly under control. Engage core throughout.
workouts.mistakes: Kipping/swinging, not fully extending at bottom, shrugging shoulders, incomplete range of motion.

workouts.name: Barbell Squat
workouts.description: The master movement. Builds legs, glutes, and core while boosting testosterone.
workouts.form: Bar on upper traps. Feet shoulder-width, toes slightly out. Squat until parallel or below. Drive through heels to stand. Keep chest up, knees tracking toes.
workouts.mistakes: Knee cave, heels rising, rounding lower back, not reaching depth, forward lean.

workouts.name: Overhead Press
workouts.description: The definitive shoulder builder. Develops all three deltoid heads and core stability.
workouts.form: Bar at collar bone. Hands just outside shoulders. Press overhead to lockout. Slightly lean back at top. Lower to chin level. Brace core hard.
workouts.mistakes: Excessive back arch, flaring elbows, pressing in front of body, losing core tension.

workouts.name: Barbell Curl
workouts.description: Classic bicep builder. Isolates the biceps for maximum peak and size.
workouts.form: Stand with barbell, underhand grip. Keep elbows at sides. Curl to shoulder height. Squeeze at top. Lower slowly. No swinging.
workouts.mistakes: Swinging torso, elbows drifting forward, partial reps, using too much weight.

workouts.name: Plank
workouts.description: The foundation of core strength. Builds endurance and stability for all lifts.
workouts.form: Forearms on ground, elbows under shoulders. Body straight from head to heels. Squeeze glutes and abs. Neutral spine. Look at floor.
workouts.mistakes: Hips too high or low, holding breath, sagging midsection, shrugging shoulders.

workouts.name: Dumbbell Fly
workouts.description: Stretches and isolates the chest for full muscle development and definition.
workouts.form: Lie on bench with dumbbells above chest. Arms slightly bent. Lower in wide arc. Squeeze chest to bring weights back up. Feel the stretch.
workouts.mistakes: Arms too straight (injury risk), going too heavy, not feeling the stretch, incomplete range.

workouts.name: Deadlift
workouts.description: The ultimate full-body movement. Builds raw power, mass, and functional strength.
workouts.form: Bar over mid-foot. Hip-width stance. Grip just outside legs. Push floor away. Bar stays close to body. Lockout hips and knees simultaneously.
workouts.mistakes: Bar drifting forward, rounding lower back, jerking the bar, hyperextending at lockout.

workouts.name: Leg Press
workouts.description: Machine-based quad and glute builder. Great for adding volume safely.
workouts.form: Feet shoulder-width on platform. Lower until 90-degree knee angle. Press through full sole of foot. Do not lock knees at top. Control descent.
workouts.mistakes: Knees caving in, locking knees, feet too low (knee strain), rounding lower back.

equipment.name: Treadmill
equipment.howToUse: Step on belt, start at slow pace, gradually increase speed/incline. Maintain upright posture, swing arms naturally. Use safety clip at all times.
equipment.safetyTips: Always clip the safety key to your clothing. Start slow to warm up. Don't look down while running. Step to side rails when stopping.

equipment.name: Barbell Bench
equipment.howToUse: Set J-hooks at proper height. Lie flat, eyes under bar. Unrack with arms locked. Lower to mid-chest, press explosively. Re-rack when done.
equipment.safetyTips: Always use a spotter for heavy sets. Use collars to secure plates. Keep feet flat on floor. Never attempt a 1RM alone.

equipment.name: Squat Rack
equipment.howToUse: Set safety bars at hip height. Position bar on upper traps. Unrack, step back two steps. Squat, rack when done. Never step forward to rack.
equipment.safetyTips: Set safety bars correctly before every set. Use a spotter. Learn to bail safely with safety bars. Warm up thoroughly before heavy loads.

equipment.name: Lat Pulldown
equipment.howToUse: Adjust thigh pads to secure legs. Overhand grip slightly wider than shoulders. Pull bar to upper chest. Lean back slightly. Control the weight up.
equipment.safetyTips: Don't swing excessively. Avoid pulling behind the neck. Keep core tight. Use appropriate weight — you should be able to control the negative.

equipment.name: Dumbbells
equipment.howToUse: Select appropriate weight. Keep back neutral on all exercises. Use full range of motion. Return to rack after use — heaviest weights on lowest pegs.
equipment.safetyTips: Use collars if using adjustable dumbbells. Don't drop weights unnecessarily. Keep clear of feet. Use gym chalk for heavy sets.

equipment.name: Leg Press Machine
equipment.howToUse: Sit with back flat against pad. Place feet shoulder-width on platform. Release safety handles. Lower slowly, press back up. Re-engage safety before exiting.
equipment.safetyTips: Never fully lock out knees. Keep back against pad throughout. Always use safety handles. Don't place hands on knees during movement.
